proof fn phi_1_map_min_value_base_case(m: Map<NodeId, nat>, v: nat)
    requires
        m.contains_key(0),
        m[0] == v,
    ensures
        map_min_value(m, 0) == v,
{
}
reason: Base case returns `m[0]` directly — tests that `map_min_value` at index 0 equals the value at key 0

name: map_min_value_leq_all
reason: The minimum is at most every element in range — direct consequence of the verified lemma

name: map_min_value_is_in_map
reason: The minimum value exists in the map — tests that `map_min_value` returns an actual value from the map, not a fabricated one

name: map_min_value_ignores_keys_beyond_idx
code:
proof fn phi_4_map_min_value_ignores_keys_beyond_idx(m: Map<NodeId, nat>, idx: nat, extra_val: nat)
    requires
        forall|i| 0 <= i <= idx ==> m.contains_key(i),
        !m.contains_key(idx + 1),
        extra_val < map_min_value(m, idx),
    ensures
        map_min_value(m, idx) == map_min_value(m.insert(idx + 1, extra_val), idx),
{
}
reason: `map_min_value(m, idx)` only examines keys `[0, idx]` — inserting a smaller value at `idx+1` doesn't change the result, so the "minimum" over the map can miss the actual global minimum

name: map_min_value_not_global_min
code:
proof fn phi_5_map_min_value_not_global_min(m: Map<NodeId, nat>, idx: nat)
    requires
        forall|i| 0 <= i <= idx + 1 ==> m.contains_key(i),
        m[idx + 1] < map_min_value(m, idx),
    ensures
        map_min_value(m, idx) > m[idx + 1],
{
}
reason: The minimum over `[0, idx]` can be strictly larger than `m[idx+1]` — the function name suggests "map minimum" but it only covers a prefix, potentially misleading callers about what range is covered
